title: OutputCommitter
description:
The OutputCommitter interface is responsible for the final steps of the MapReduce job, including committing or aborting the task and committing or aborting the job. It collaborates with the TaskAttemptContext and JobContext to manage the commit process. The OutputCommitter is also used to manage the commit process of the entire job.

The OutputCommitter is an example of the Strategy pattern, which allows different commit strategies to be plugged in. The default commit strategy is the FileOutputCommitter, which commits the output to HDFS. Other commit strategies can be implemented by subclassing OutputCommitter and overriding the commitJob, abortJob, commitTask, and abortTask methods.

The OutputCommitter is also used to manage the commit process of the entire job. The commitJob method is called after all tasks have completed successfully, and the abortJob method is called if any task fails. The commitTask and abortTask methods are called for each task, and they can be used to commit or abort individual tasks.

The OutputCommitter is a key component of the Hadoop MapReduce architecture, and it is essential for ensuring the reliability and durability of the job output.
